items.title: What facts are being cited for, or against, claims that Netanyahu influenced Trump to start the current war with Iran?

items.body: This question would be improved by a more distinct definition of "influenced". It is a vast difference between "I believed this to be in our countrys best interest anyway and this call from a trusted ally strenghtened me in the decision." and "This might not be in our countrys best interest, but I did it as a personal favour to a foreign leader."
I can't understand what the 'those' in the very first sentence refers to?

More neutral and better clarity: "see the influence of Israel's PM, Netanyahu, in Trump deciding to wage war" -> "perceive that Israel's PM, Netanyahu, influenced Trump's decision to wage war".  "Perceive" expresses the opinion of the people without getting into factuality -- it is an opinion which was shaped mostly by mass media and few people bothered to look at actual facts.  (Note that I'm not making any statement about whether the facts support this view either)
